registration_intent.domain_class: viral capsid self-assembly architecture / non-covalent thermodynamic+kinetic shell formation
registration_intent.rationale: HEXA-WEAVE registers write-side multi-strand molecular design composition (P up to 10^4 strand bundle, primary constraint Landauer x NP-search ceiling, primary oracle AlphaFold-class fold inference, covalent bond regime). HEXA-NANOBOT registers single molecular nano-machine architecture (10^0-10^2 atoms per actuator, primary constraint Brownian thermal floor at 310K, primary oracle molecular-dynamics simulation, mechanical bond regime). HEXA-RIBOZYME registers catalytic RNA architecture (single ribozyme active site ~12-30 nt catalytic core, primary constraint diffusion-limit ceiling k_cat / K_M ~ 10^8 M^-1 s^-1, primary oracle chemical-kinetics simulation + RNA-secondary-structure prediction, catalytic-cleavage bond regime). HEXA-VIROCAPSID registers a fourth orthogonal sub-axis covering viral capsid self-assembly architecture (single capsid shell 60-420 subunits / 10^5-10^7 atoms, primary constraint kinetic-trap ceiling on aberrant-assembly fraction (Zlotnick 2003 nucleation-elongation), primary oracle Caspar-Klug 1962 geometric + Zlotnick 2003 kinetic + cryo-EM atomic-model, non-covalent inter-CP interface bond regime). The four-way split is genus-level: WEAVE composes strands (covalent); NANOBOT actuates atoms (mechanical); RIBOZYME catalyses bonds (catalytic); VIROCAPSID self-assembles shells (thermodynamic+kinetic). Caspar-Klug 1962 / Crick-Watson 1956 / Rossmann 1985 / Harrison 1978 / Liljas 1982 / Zlotnick 2003 / Twarock 2016 / Bruinsma 2003 define the capsid-architecture literature corpus. Distinct from AlphaFold-class read-side prediction, from IsoDDE drug-design, from DNA-origami nano-machines, from CRISPR-Cas RNP gene editing, and from clinical virology/vaccine domains under the life axis. Closes the biology-axis tetrahedron (composition + actuation + catalysis + assembly) at 4 sisters.

n6_invariant_mapping.sigma_6_eq_12: T=1 icosahedral capsid 12 vertex (STRUCTURAL-EXACT — stronger than hexa-ribozyme STRUCTURAL-APPROXIMATE; Caspar-Klug 1962 quasi-equivalence; T-number = h^2 + h*k + k^2 with (h,k)=(1,0) yields T=1; vertex count = 12 is topological invariant of the icosahedron at every T-number; Liljas 1982 STNV is the canonical T=1 60-subunit case)
n6_invariant_mapping.tau_6_eq_4: 4 assembly states (free-CP / pentamer / hexamer / capsid); Zlotnick 2003 nucleation-elongation kinetic model factors capsid assembly into 4 macroscopic regimes
n6_invariant_mapping.J_2_eq_24: octahedral group O (order 24) as natural subgroup of icosahedral group I (order 60); Hessenberg-Cole subgroup-lattice analysis of A_5 shows O acts on 5-inscribed-cubes of icosahedron via outer 5-cycle action; J_2 = 24 quotient appears as per-cube pose-equivalence class
n6_invariant_mapping.phi_6_eq_2: binary closed/open shell outcome (sealed shell with no aperture > 1 nm; cargo inaccessible / aperture-bearing shell with portal / pentamer eviction / triggered opening; cargo accessible)
